title: asarum canadense var. reflexum
intro: Asarum canadense var. reflexum, also known as Canadian wild ginger or Canadian ginger, is a type of flowering plant in the birthwort family that is native to eastern North America. It is a perennial plant with heart-shaped leaves and produces small, greenish-brown flowers in late spring. The roots and rhizomes of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing nausea and improving digestion. The plant's roots and rhizomes can be prepared as a decoction, tincture, or powder for use in herbal remedies, and are also sometimes used as a spice or in cooking.
